steps.title: Identify the condition and relevant table entries
steps.content: At least 10 years old means the 10–19 and 20+ columns combined. From the table: 30 participants aged 10–19, and 30 aged 20+, for a total of 60 participants at least 10 years old. Within these 60, Group A has 14 + 9 = 23 participants.

steps.title: Calculate the conditional probability
steps.content:
Conditional probability restricts the sample space to those meeting the condition, then counts favorable outcomes within it:

$$P(\text{Group A} \mid \text{age} \geq 10) = \frac{\text{Group A and age} \geq 10}{\text{Total with age} \geq 10} = \frac{23}{60}$$

As a decimal: $23 \div 60 = 0.3833...$
